name: Blender Double Fudge Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. Grease and flour a 9x9x2" baking pan.
3. Measure nuts into the blender; chop on low speed. Set nuts aside.
4. In the blender, measure the remaining ingredients except chocolate pieces.
5. Blend for 30 seconds on high speed, stopping occasionally to scrape sides with a rubber spatula. The batter may be slightly lumpy.
6. Pour the batter into the prepared pan.
7. Bake for about 35 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
8. Immediately sprinkle chocolate pieces over the cake.
9. Place the baking sheet over the pan so that contained heat will soften the chocolate pieces. Spread softened chocolate evenly over the cake and sprinkle with reserved nuts.
10. Serve warm.
To prepare cake with an electric mixer, measure all ingredients except nuts and chocolate pieces into a large mixing bowl. Mix for 1/2 minute on low speed, scraping the bowl constantly. Beat for 3 minutes on high speed, scraping the bowl occasionally. Bake as directed.
If using self-rising flour, omit baking powder and salt.
